title: arctostaphylos glandulosa f. adamsii
intro: Arctostaphylos glandulosa f. adamsii, also known as Bigberry Manzanita, is a subspecies of the evergreen shrub Arctostaphylos glandulosa, native to the western United States. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its high levels of vitamin C, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Bigberry Manzanita have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bigberry Manzanita can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
